spec.reasoning: The instruction references specific cell addresses (B1, B2, D1), column D, and the label 'Result:' — so those must NOT be changed. I only rename 'Input:' to 'Entry:' (same descriptiveness level, colon-labeled to colon-labeled) since 'Input:' is not referenced in the instruction at all. Since 'Input:' also appears as data values in column A (rows 2, 5, 6, 9, 10), I include data_value_renames to update those occurrences. The sheet is renamed from 'Sheet1' to 'DataSheet' which is safe since the instruction doesn't reference the sheet name. A distractor column 'Notes:' is added after the last column (column E), which won't affect column D's position or any cell references in the instruction. I explicitly avoid renaming 'Result:' (referenced in instruction as 'Result:'), 'Description A'/'Description B' (referenced in instruction), or 'Single A'/'Single B'/'Multiple' (these are answer values). This perturbation changes surface anchors (column name, sheet name, adds distractor) without altering task logic or difficulty.
